notes: T2.5 established the repository validate workflow, local lint enforcement for CI shape, and repo-local PR lifecycle guidance before T3 product work.
T3 is the first product task that must use the lifecycle-gated worker chain and PR lifecycle evidence.

T2.6 established the 12-level test matrix mapping, CI lane mapping, CodeQL workflow, scanner lint checks, docs parity, output contract, release integrity, provenance evidence, systems-thinking, TDD/ADR, performance, reliability, and fail-closed rules.

All current task packets now carry ci_lane_refs, test_matrix_refs, security_scanner_gates, engineering_policy_refs, and architecture_guidance_refs; future task packets must keep them or record an approved exception.

T2.6 planning now also preserves Factory prd-to-plan and execution-compiler fields: public contract map, docs/OSS readiness, test matrix wiring, changelog/versioning intent, contract impact, ADR/TDD posture, cost/perf, failure hypotheses, and semantic invariants.
T1 established the pinned Go CLI skeleton, command-result envelope, exit-code constants, config defaults, and validation lanes.
Record/replay/report scope remains partial until schemas, init/check, record, replay, and report tasks close.
T2 established executable Phase 0 schema files and schema validation coverage for workflow, evidence, cassette, proof, command-result, redaction, and related artifact models.
MVP live eval is not closed until both OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic provider adapters are implemented or an explicit human-approved scope exception is recorded.

full_mvp_closure_rule: The MVP is complete only when every required acceptance group is implemented or explicitly accepted with documented delivery debt by a human owner.
